meta.title: Onboarding Benchmark Matrix
meta.description: 1:1 comparison of onboarding, install, setup knobs, progressive disclosure, migration, and first-run UX across Lev, Hermes, OpenClaw, Oh My OpenAgent, Oh My Codex, and Oh My ClaudeCode.

props.title: Onboarding, Install, and Project-Init Matrix
props.subtitle: Evidence-backed comparison across Lev, Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, Oh My OpenAgent, Oh My Codex, and Oh My ClaudeCode using workshop intake docs plus two local lev.exec scouts.

props.label: TAKEAWAYS
props.title: What Each Platform Optimizes For
props.subtitle: The best onboarding experience depends on whether the system is a gateway, a plugin, or a workflow layer.

props.title: 🦞 OpenClaw
props.content: Most complete operator onboarding. `openclaw onboard` plus a very deep `doctor`/migration surface, daemon install, control-plane runbook, and explicit security defaults.

props.title: ☤ Hermes
props.content: Hybrid script + wizard + gateway model. Strong migration story from OpenClaw, profile-aware homes, and broad provider portability, but less consumer-polished than OpenClaw.

props.title: 🧠 OpenAgent
props.content: Most explicit provider questionnaire. Install guide asks subscription-by-subscription questions, drives auth setup, and writes agent-specific model config rather than one global provider default.

props.title: ⚙️ OMX
props.content: Best scoped setup. `omx setup` clearly owns prompts, skills, config, `AGENTS.md`, `.omx/` runtime state, and user vs project scope. Onboarding cleanly hands off into the canonical workflow.

props.title: 🧩 OMC
props.content: Fastest plugin-first quick start, but the install story is split: marketplace/plugin path, npm path, local setup, and variant repos all coexist. Good UX, weaker source-of-truth discipline.

props.title: 🜂 Lev
props.content: Cleanest conceptual split: `lev install` for machine infra, `lev init` for project bootstrap, `install()` SDK for lifecycle orchestration. Least productized operator surface today.

props.label: MATRIX A
props.title: Install and First-Run Surface
props.subtitle: Primary install entry, setup path, scope model, and what happens before the user can do real work.

rows.lev: `lev install` machine infra; `lev init` project bootstrap
rows.omc: plugin marketplace install; npm path also documented

rows.lev: intended package split; no polished checklist yet
rows.hermes: git only for quick install; script provisions uv, Python, Node deps
rows.omx: Node 20+, Codex CLI installed and authenticated, tmux/psmux for team mode
rows.omc: Claude Code plugin path; npm package optional depending on doc

rows.openclaw: `openclaw onboard` guides gateway, workspace, channels, skills
rows.openagent: install guide for humans and LLMs; explicit step sequence

rows.omc: stefandevo installer is interactive; plugin path is low-ceremony but not strongly headless

rows.openagent: rich questionnaire: Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, Copilot, OpenCode Zen, Z.ai, OpenCode Go
rows.omc: Claude first; optional Codex/Gemini worker CLIs; OpenClaw/ask integration env
none in mainline; stefan variant wires hooks and MCP into Claude settings

rows.hermes: venv, `.env`, symlink, synced skills, optional wizard run
rows.openagent: plugin registration in `opencode.json`; agent models configured; auth steps queued
rows.omc: global or local setup files; plugin path plus `.omc/` and optional local `CLAUDE.md`

props.title: 📌 OMC Variant Note
props.content: The matrix uses the Yeachan-Heo OMC mainline as the primary column. The stefan-devo fork adds a real shell installer with explicit per-project vs system-wide mode, MCP wiring, research-tool setup, permission mode selection, and update/uninstall scripts.

props.label: MATRIX B
props.title: Doctor, Migration, Progressive Disclosure, and Knobs
props.subtitle: The differentiator is not just install. It’s what each platform does after install to keep users on the rails.

rows.openclaw: best-in-class `doctor` with repair, force, deep scan, legacy migrations, config normalization
rows.openagent: `bunx oh-my-opencode doctor` verifies plugin, config, models, environment
rows.omc: `/omc-doctor`; stefan variant uses update/install scripts and settings repair

rows.hermes: OpenClaw migration built in; dry-run, preset, overwrite
rows.openclaw: doctor performs many legacy config/state migrations
rows.openagent: legacy package-name compatibility and config basename migration
rows.omx: mainline docs include setup-scope migration and uninstall/cleanup semantics
rows.omc: MIGRATION guide across major versions and runtime shifts

rows.lev: clean machine vs project split, but thin UX
rows.hermes: quick install → setup → chat; CLI vs messaging quick reference
rows.openclaw: README pushes one command, then docs split into wizard/config/runbook/doctor/security
rows.openagent: humans paste a prompt; agent fetches install guide; detailed step 0 questionnaire
rows.omx: main path separated from operator surfaces; setup/doctor/HUD are explicitly secondary
rows.omc: 3-step quick start, then optional deep-interview/team runtime and magic keywords

rows.hermes: profile-scoped homes, not project bootstrap oriented
rows.openagent: user config and project `.opencode/` config both supported
rows.omc: mainline local vs global setup; stefan variant has explicit per-project/system-wide install selector

rows.hermes: skills synced under `~/.hermes/skills`; workspace instructions migratable
rows.openclaw: skills and workspace setup via onboard
rows.openagent: plugin config plus compatibility-aware file names
rows.omc: global or local `CLAUDE.md`; stefan variant merges hooks and MCP into settings.json

rows.openagent: doctor/local version checks; upgrade path less centralized
rows.omc: npm upgrade or `/plugin marketplace update omc` then rerun setup
plugin uninstall path; npm/global uninstall docs are inconsistent

rows.knobs: `openclaw onboard --install-daemon`; `doctor --yes --repair --force --non-interactive --deep`; update channels; DM pairing defaults
`--no-tui`, `--claude`, `--openai`, `--gemini`, `--copilot`, `--opencode-zen`, `--zai-coding-plan`, `--opencode-go`; auth login flows and plugin config overrides

plugin marketplace path, local `omc-setup --local`, `CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS`, optional Codex/Gemini worker CLIs; stefan variant adds per-project/system-wide mode, research tools, permission mode, MCP merge

props.label: EVIDENCE
props.title: Grounding and Variant Notes
props.subtitle: These are the highest-signal local sources I used to build the matrix.

props.title: 🦞 OpenClaw Evidence
props.content: `workshop/intake/openclaw/README.md` lines 26-31, 118-157, 167-170; `workshop/intake/openclaw/docs/gateway/doctor.md` lines 11-93; gateway runbook for service lifecycle.

props.title: 🧠 OpenAgent Evidence
props.content: `workshop/intake/oh-my-openagent/docs/guide/installation.md` lines 14-205 and README uninstall/config sections at lines 272-340.

props.title: ⚙️ OMX Evidence
props.content: `workshop/intake/oh-my-codex/README.md` lines 48-165; `docs/getting-started.html` lines 25-65; translated README sections with scope and generated files at `README.de.md` lines 95-118 and 182-199.

props.title: 🧩 OMC Evidence
props.content: Mainline: `workshop/intake/oh-my-claudecode/README.md` lines 45-169. Variant installer surface: `workshop/intake/stefandevo-omc/install.sh` lines 29-67, 77-107, 221-256, 296-345, 424-448.

props.title: 🤖 Scout Corroboration
props.content: Local `lev exec` scout report completed for the Hermes/OpenClaw/OpenAgent cluster at `/tmp/lev-scout-cluster-a.md`. A second scout was launched for the OMX/OMC cluster and direct intake evidence was used regardless.
